evals.prompt: J'ai reçu cette convocation d'AG : /Users/montimage/buildspace/luongnv89/francais-skills/resources/2-residence-du-verger-2026.pdf. Peux-tu me dire l'essentiel et ce à quoi je dois faire attention avant de voter ?
evals.expected_output: The skill ingests the PDF, writes an interactive HTML report to the current working directory, then briefs the user with meeting date, vote-by-correspondence deadline, budget Y-o-Y, and a 3-5 bullet 'what to pay attention to' recap.
expectations: An HTML file is written to the current working directory matching the pattern ag-le-verger-a-palaiseau-2026.html (residence name and year 2026)
The HTML contains a <script type="application/json" id="ag-data"> block with a parseable JSON object
The embedded JSON has schema_version '1.1' (or later) and source.pdf_path set to the absolute path of the source PDF
The embedded JSON has residence ('LE VERGER À PALAISEAU' or similar), meeting.date '2026-06-16', syndic containing 'IMMO DE FRANCE', and owner.tantiemes 233
The embedded JSON includes a budget object with n_plus_2_eur 57950 and delta_pct approximately 17.4
The embedded JSON has all 19 resolutions in the resolutions[] array, with Résolution 11 amount_eur 57950 and Résolution 16 amount_eur 350
Most résolutions in the resolutions[] array carry a non-empty pages array of positive integers, and budget.pages is set to a non-empty array (page numbers may vary by extraction)
The embedded JSON has a briefing[] array of 3-7 items with at least one high-severity item about the budget jump
The HTML loads Tailwind CSS (cdn.tailwindcss.com) and Chart.js (cdn.jsdelivr.net) via <script> tags
The HTML contains canvas elements for chart-budget, chart-resolutions, chart-lines, and chart-travaux
The rendered HTML body contains at least one 'page-chip' element citing a page in the source PDF
The response identifies the meeting as 'mardi 16 juin 2026 à 19h00' at the Salle de réunion de la copropriété in Palaiseau
The response states the vote-by-correspondence deadline as 12/06/2026
The response highlights the budget jump from 49 350 € to 57 950 € (approximately +17 %)
The response flags Résolution 16 (350 € carrelage chez locataire) and Résolution 19 (bâches publicitaires) as unusual items worth attention
The response is in French because the question was in French and points the user at the HTML file

evals.prompt: Here's my AG convocation: /Users/montimage/buildspace/luongnv89/francais-skills/resources/17-rue-philippe-de-dangeau-2025.pdf. When's the meeting, what's the budget, and what do I need to watch out for?
evals.expected_output: The skill ingests the VPàt Immo formatted PDF, writes an HTML report to the working directory with language='en' embedded JSON, and briefs the user in English while preserving French legal terms.
expectations: An HTML file is written to the current working directory matching ag-17-rue-philippe-de-dangeau-2025.html
The HTML contains a <script type="application/json" id="ag-data"> block with a parseable JSON object
The embedded JSON has schema_version '1.1' (or later) and source.pdf_path set to the absolute path of the source PDF
The embedded JSON has language 'en' (matching the English question), syndic containing 'VPàt' or 'VPAT', and meeting.date '2025-05-14'
The embedded JSON has budget.n_plus_1_eur and budget.n_plus_2_eur both equal to 24000 (flat budget)
The embedded JSON has multiple travaux entries or résolutions tagged 'travaux' covering chemisage colonne d'eaux usées, remplacement tronçon fonte, and ravalement
Most résolutions carry a non-empty pages array; the briefing items reference back to source pages
The response identifies the meeting as Wednesday 14 May 2025 at 18:00, 52 Rue Saint Charles in Versailles
The response identifies the budget as 24 000 € for both periods (flat year over year)
The response flags Résolution 21 (convention d'honoraires de Maître COGNY for ~6 120 €) as a notable legal item
The response is in English because the question was in English
French legal terms (syndic, travaux, tantièmes, Article 24/25) are preserved verbatim in italics or quotes rather than translated
The response tells the user to open the HTML file in their browser for the interactive report

evals.expected_output: With no path argument, the skill globs the current working directory for ag-*.html report files, lists any matches, and asks the user to pick one or provide a new PDF path. If none found in CWD, it may also offer to look at legacy ~/.ag-copro/summaries/*.md files.
expectations: The skill does NOT immediately try to ingest a PDF
The skill globs the current working directory for files matching ag-*.html and lists them (or states the directory has no such files)
The skill asks the user to either pick an existing HTML report or provide a path to a new PDF
The skill does not invent or hallucinate residences

evals.prompt: I already have ag-le-verger-a-palaiseau-2026.html in this folder — can you tell me which résolutions involve actual money?
evals.expected_output: The skill loads the existing HTML, extracts and parses the embedded JSON data block, then lists résolutions where amount_eur is not null. Does NOT re-read the source PDF.
expectations: The skill reads the HTML file and extracts the <script type="application/json" id="ag-data"> block
The skill parses the JSON and filters resolutions where amount_eur is not null
The response lists at least Résolution 9 (syndic, 8 292,85 €), 10 (budget actualisé), 11 (budget 2027, 57 950 €), 15 (assistance, 384 €), 16 (carrelage, 350 €), 17 (persiennes, 2 000 €)
The skill does NOT re-read the source PDF
The skill cites résolution numbers, and includes the source page (from each résolution's pages array) when listing résolutions
